finding.issue: Non-dict entries in the node list only increment unknown (→ DEGRADED at worst), never fabricated, despite being invalid node records in an integrity node set.
finding.why_it_matters: Garbage/corrupt node payloads are treated more gently than an illegal status string. An adversary or buggy producer can dilute the set with non-dicts without tripping FABRICATION_DETECTED (status becomes DEGRADED only if other nodes are clean of fabrication).
finding.adversarial_test: nodes=['not-a-dict'] alone and mixed with fully evidenced valid nodes; record integrity_status and fabricated_detected.

finding.issue: classify_active_leases parses expires_at as local wall time: strips Z then uses time.mktime(time.strptime(...)), which is local-time conversion, not UTC.
finding.why_it_matters: UTC lease expiry becomes wrong by the host offset. Leases can be treated as active when already expired (or the reverse), so capacity/active counts and STALE_REQUIRES_RECOVERY classification can be false.
finding.adversarial_test: Create a *.lock with expires_at a few minutes in the past UTC (Z suffix) on a host with TZ offset ≠ 0; assert is_expired classification flips incorrectly vs true UTC now.

finding.issue: Admin/recovery lease detection is substring match on task_id including very short tokens MUT and EXP (also TIMEOUT, DUP, etc.).
finding.why_it_matters: Real worker task_ids containing those substrings (e.g. EXPORT, EXPERIMENT, COMPUTE, TIMEOUT-HANDLING) are counted as recovery/injection, not workers.
